newsSince: Around June 5 Advanced Energy launched its ADH 800V DC-DC converters for AI data-center rack power (8kW peak, 98.2% peak efficiency, megawatt-capable rack solutions), and on ~June 14 filed an omnibus shelf registration and announced redemption of its remaining 2.50% 2028 convertible notes. Management targets 2026 data-center revenue growth in the mid-30s%. The 800V optionality was largely known on June 9; the +18% move since June 9 reflects renewed AI-power enthusiasm and the financing/capital-structure actions more than a new fundamental shift.

sources.label: StockTitan: AEIS 800V DC AI data-center converters
Sahm Capital: AEIS up 20% after shelf filing and 800V launch

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: the ~June 5 800V AI rack-power launch makes the optionality more concrete, a mild fundamental positive. But +66% YTD, $15B mid-cap visibility and the convert-redemption/shelf financing mean residual alpha is largely captured. Hold in the Monitor/benchmark bucket with a small uptick.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: ADH 800V DC-DC platform for megawatt AI rack power; data-center growth mid-30s%
verdicts.primaryRisk: Already-rerated mid-cap; shelf filing signals potential dilution overhang
verdicts.bull: 800V/megawatt-rack power conversion positions AEIS as a hard-to-substitute AI-rack power supplier as racks move to higher-voltage architectures.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI-power optionality, but +66% YTD and mid-cap visibility mean much is priced; financing actions add an overhang.
verdicts.bear: Crowded AI-power trade; shelf registration hints at dilution and the rerating leaves limited residual alpha.
